raw_input:
[5.0/5] Easy to handle Sooth ❤️ [5.0/5] A great purchase Got this for my husband for Christmas. He’s had it a lil over a month and uses it about 2-3 times a week. It hasn’t rusted and has been holding up! He really loves this style and other razors we’ve seen have been $50+ so it was very worth it and a great buy! [5.0/5] High Quality Adjustable Razor Awesome quality razor very well made. Adjustment is very smooth and it is a great shaver with a quality feel. I ordered this and the Qshave at the same time and they are identical razors, other than the Qshave comes with a small cleaning brush. This is advertised as a "long" handle version but it is the exact same size as the Qshave. Its well worth the price and I will probably be buying some for gifts, given the low price and high quality. I love it. [5.0/5] ... this razor for over a week now and get awesome results. Gets a little to get used to I've used this razor for over a week now and get awesome results. Gets a little to get used to, but the shave is close. I would definitely buy another one if i need to replace this one. [1.0/5] Not going to use This razor seemed to have a lot of promise but failed to make it. First off there are a few good points. The material seems fine with only a few minor flaws in the base material that are not seen unless specifically looked for. The finish is fine with no bubbling or flaking. The numbering is clear and easy to read.
The rest;
The head is way too big, nearly impossible to get hairs in area around nostrils.
The head does not hold the blade edge properly and causes flex/chatter.
The blade edge reveal seems incorrect. The blade edge seems too even with the comb so that when you adjust the razor you just get a wider gap that lets skin in but not more edge reveal. Just ends up harder to dial in a comfortable shave.
The head moves around too much causing uneven gap that you constantly have to correct.
Balance of the razor is terrible.

The included blades are acceptable, on par with your average blade but nothing I would purchase. [4.0/5] Third time was the charm Well I had tried two other versions of this same product, Q-Shave and River Lake and, despite the fact that they all likely came from the same Chinese manufacturer, this Manion razor was the only one that had a balanced gap alignment and blade reveal. So I lucked out, on my third try, before completely giving up on these Merkur Futur clones altogether. The packaging on this one is very basic. As far as I know, it does not come in a set with stand included. This one comes with 5 Dorco stainless steel blades. There are no instructions and no operational warnings enclosed. Had I not ordered the other brands, I am not sure I would have understood the proper way to remove the cover and load the blade. Please, please, whatever you do, DO NOT change the blade or adjust the blade gap with wet hands (you need to hold the head while you turn the handle to adjust the blade gap). Just so happens I neglected to adequately dry my hands, I went to adjust the blade gap, my hand slipped and I sliced my finger. Lesson learned.

As on all other models, including the Futur, settings range from #1-6. I would consider the shave to be very aggressive at settings above #2. The finish is smooth and unmarred. As others have mentioned, the handle can get slippery when wet. So just be careful and grasp the handle at the indent below the head for better control. [5.0/5] Excellent finish and good balance. This is very good quality considering the economical price. The finish is smooth and the weight is balanced. [4.0/5] Ideal first double edge razor for Men or Women with adjustable setting from novice to expert I always wanted to try the Futura and my daily shaver is the Merkur HD which is an ideal choice for people that want to shave one pass and get to work, teamed up with Feather Blades black label 5 packs or yellow label 10 packs.
The Futura is almost $100 so that wasn't going to happen unless I knew I would like it so when I saw this clone for 12.64 I couldn't resist and I'm very happy with it. The design really is unique and but seems to work on this knockoff which appears identical to the Merkur version. The blades "dorco" it comes with are terrible at least for me and once I put my feathers in it provided a great one pass shave on setting 3.
My one knock on the razor is what makes it so handy especially for beginners the bulky head, it is near impossible for me to get right up tight under my nose so I use my HD for that otherwise it is a great razor. And I'm buying three more for presents. The Merkur version is supposedly heavier but that might almost be too heavy, this Razor to me is heavy enough, almost too heavy.

Women if you are considering a double edge razor this is the one it will give you great results safely and easily just put the razor on setting one. after the you recapture the razor cost for about 10-12 dollars a year you can have an ideal body shaving solution [5.0/5] Wunderbar! For an adjustable straight blade, this is nearly impossible to beat. You also get 5 Dorco blades; Dorco are some of the best blades you can buy. [1.0/5] Horrible shave. Cuts and razor burn all over my face. Omg I've been using Gillette fusion for years and decided to try this and eliminate razor bumps. I now have razor burns all over my face and neck along with multiple cuts. What a waste of money and time. They don't even give directions on how to load althe blade!!! [5.0/5] A Sturdy Built Adjustable Double Edge Razor 🪒 I purchased the razor after I no longer could buy replacement cartridges locally for my Schick Hydro. Also, I contemplated for years about trying a double edge razor but felt nervous about cutting myself. However, since this razor is adjustable gave me the confidence to try one. At first, I used the number one position to avoid any mishaps. After a few uses, I felt confident and used the razor at the number two position. I finally found for myself the sweet spot for a close and smooth shave is slightly past the number three on the adjustment dial. In conclusion, I am happy with the razor for the price and will never use any other type of razor as long as the double edge razor exists. [5.0/5] Five Stars It’s the best safety razor I own... [2.0/5] drop it and it falls appart You have to put the razor in the lid then snap it on the base. When you want to clean it you have to take it apart the reassemble it, If you thought it would screw open and shut think again. [5.0/5] What a Bargain I have been using this razor for several weeks now and just love it. However, I'd shy away from the blades that came with it. Adjusting the blade is a breeze and it just plain looks classy. [5.0/5] Flexible shave settings; Use towel to assemble and diasseble razor to avoid cutting fingers. Pros
1) Easily adjustable aggressiveness of shave (such that less passess of the shaver over the face is needed to produce a clean shave when set closer to 6 of 6 where the safety guide is farther from the blade edge as compared to 1 of 6 where the safety guide is closer to the blade edge.

2) Silver plating of shaver works well with trig silver plated edge blades to avoid: skin irritation, skin inflammation and shaving skin leading to bleeding.

3) Sleek form.

Cons
1) Need to use a heavy face towel to remove head of shaver from body to clean blade and shaver body. Also need to use said towel to assemble shaver: On towel razor cover is first placed, then razor blade, then press razor mount attached to handle onto cover sandwiching blade. This way of assembling and disassembling razor avoids cutting fingers.

2) Razor mount on handle has two holes where debris from shaving can get caught as well as residual moiture from wet shaving. Need to rinse mount, then wipe dirh paper towel, then dry with hair dryer. [4.0/5] Good value but slippery. Just got it in the mail. Did not want to spend excess money for something I might not like, so I figured the knock-off would be the way to go for now. It feels hefty with most weight directed toward the head which I believe should help in the razor doing the work. I will say it’s very slick silky feel in the hand might be a negative. Instructions say to operate with dry hands, which I did but it seem to slip out of my hands unless gripping it well. Have not shaved with it yet but already cut my thumb while adjusting the settings with a blade installed. My dumb mistake. I was attempting to mimic adjusting during a shave session. In attempt at seeing which setting I might like. I would suggest for anyone that needed to adjust this razor to hold it upside down on a towel or washcloth then turn the handle to your desired setting. Don’t hold the top as it’s likely to slip from your holding fingers and cut you. Be careful. I’m glad I purchased this razor at the price I paid before spending way much more money on the German made one. Seems like a good razor overall for the value. [5.0/5] Gives me a predictable shave exactly when I need it ... Gives me a predictable shave exactly when I need it. It is a bit bulky on the head and the handle may seem a bit slippery but all in all it provides a quality shave. [5.0/5] Great razor that can be as gentle or aggressive as you'd like it to be This clone of the Merkur Futur adjustable was way better than I expected. I've been using it for legs and underarms, primarily, for about 4 months. I find it to be built with good quality -- it provides a close shave, adjusts smoothly, and the head seems to be set pretty even on both sides. I found no issue even when shaving more oddly-shaped hills and valleys. I feel that the razor has pretty good grip as long as your hands aren't slathered in soap. It also comes with 5 high quality Dorco blades in a little plastic dispenser that can also be used to store used blades in a separate compartment until you're ready to dispose of it.

To clean and change blades, you have to be a little careful because you need to pop the top cover of the razor's head off to do it. I grab the handle firmly and turn the razor as though it's a tiny hammer, then use the other hand to gently pop/pivot the top cover off with the thumb and index finger.

One thing I learned from YouTube is that this and other Futur clones like the QShave -- you can adjust the head's gap width further, if you prefer a more aggressive razor. When you get to level 6, squeeze the entire razor head together then twist the dial clockwise again -- you will bypass the locking mechanism and reach level 1 again. You'll see the gap widen on the head. In this "mode", level 1 technically becomes level 7 and so on. I prefer mine this way, although the razor's perfectly good as it comes out of the box. To get back to the default gap width settings, move back to level 1 (i.e. 7), squeeze the head again and twist the dial counterclockwise until you're back at level 6.

I've not ever used a real Futur so I don't know how well it mimics that one in actual performance but, from what I understand, these clone razors mostly vary from the original in weight and minor design differences (apparently the original Merkur patent expired, making way for these clones). What I can tell you is that this one's a great deal for the money.

One other thing to note: This week, I began to really understand that we have a major condensation issue in our bathroom (There's no exhaust fan and only a tiny window.) While some other metal items began to show some rust, this razor is one of the few things that doesn't have a single spot on it anywhere. Given the price, that's definitely evidence of higher quality materials than I would have expected. [4.0/5] Manion M5 Matte Silver Adjustable Razor Slipperiness issue solved by wrapping orthodontic elastics (dental rubber bands for braces) onto the handle. The razor itself has uneven blade reveal; thus one side provides a milder shave and the other side a more aggressive shave, Not a problem if used as a single edge, but the adjustability feature is mitigated. A fine value overall with regard to price / quality. [5.0/5] Close shave Closest shave I've ever had without irritation. The smooth metal finish is slippery So be careful but other than that it's solid and well built.

What I can tell you is that this one's a great deal for the money.

response: Most reviewers happy with razor. Good quality for low price. Clone of Merkur Futur. Smooth adjustment, close shave. Comes with 5 Dorco blades. Handle slippery when wet - use dry hands, grip carefully. Head bulky, hard to reach under nose. Assembly unusual - pop top off, no screw mechanism. Some units have uneven blade gap. Identical to QShave razor. Settings 1-6, aggressive above 2. Included blades not great, better blades improve shave. No instructions included - dangerous for new users. Two bad reviews: one got cuts and razor burn, one said head too big and blade gap wrong. Good rust resistance. Great value compared to real Futur at $100.
